title: Local obituary: Riley J. Sergi, Central Catholic graduate from Dracut
author: Boston.com
bullets: Riley J. Sergi, a 28-year-old Central Catholic graduate from Dracut, died from injuries sustained in a car accident on June 28, 2026.
He was studying to become an electrician at Whittier Tech and had a passion for gaming, music, sports, and cooking.
Riley is survived by his parents, brother, extended family, and best friend; memorial donations suggested to Roadway Safety Foundation.

title: A seal pup was riding a boogie board off the coast of Maine, because of course it was
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A seal pup was spotted on a boogie board off Old Orchard Beach, Maine, during Fourth of July weekend.
The seal hijacked the board from a tourist and nearly returned to its colony on Stratton Island.
Captain Marco Lamothe of Keeper Charters shared photos of the seal, noting such occurrences, while rare, do happen.

title: ‘Huzzah!’: Boston’s sailing community is ready to welcome the Tall Ships
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 250 begins July 11 with a Grand Parade of Sail showcasing nearly 50 Tall Ships.
Massachusetts Bay Sailing Association and yacht clubs will organize flotillas to watch the Tall Ships parade.
Courageous Sailing Center and Community Boating Inc. offer educational activities during the Tall Ships event.

title: ‘5 July was equal to 1 April’: Belgian coach, FA not happy with FIFA decision on Folarin Balogun
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: FIFA reversed Folarin Balogun's red card suspension, allowing him to play in the World Cup match against Belgium.
Belgium's coach Rudi Garcia expressed displeasure, comparing July 5 to April Fools' Day following FIFA's decision reversal.
The Royal Belgian Football Association criticized the decision, raising concerns about fairness and FIFA's handling of the appeal.

title: Cedric Maxwell has a message for Jaylen Brown: Don’t bash the Celtics on the way out
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cedric Maxwell advises Jaylen Brown to avoid criticizing the Celtics after his trade to Philadelphia.
Brown was denied entry at the Celtics' practice facility following his trade after 10 seasons in Boston.
Jayson Tatum publicly expressed gratitude for playing alongside Brown, wishing him the best in his next career chapter.

title: A black bear is apparently making its way across southeastern Mass.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A black bear has been reported across several towns in southeastern Massachusetts, including Stoughton and Avon.
Local police advise the public to keep a safe distance if they spot the bear due to safety concerns.
Recent bear sightings were also reported in Canton and Randolph, prompting local officials to issue warnings.

title: Mass. truck driver charged in killing of Penn. trooper
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Michael Bon, 33, from Brockton, charged with homicide and other offenses after fatal crash in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania.
Trooper Michael Pahira Jr. died following a crash on Interstate 81 when Bon's truck struck the vehicle he was inspecting.
Bon is held on $700,000 bail and scheduled for a preliminary hearing on July 16 in Schuylkill County.

title: Shootings leave 2 dead, another 11 injured in Boston as councilors call for ‘public safety summit’
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston experienced five shootings on Saturday night, resulting in 13 victims, including two fatalities in Roxbury.
City Councilors Erin Murphy and Ed Flynn are urging a public safety summit following the recent violence.
Councilors call for increased police staffing and a summer safety plan amid low staffing levels and recent incidents.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about the potential for heavy rain and flooding ahead
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston forecasts predict heavy rain and potential flash flooding in Southern New England into Tuesday, impacting Massachusetts areas like Boston.
The National Weather Service expects 1-3 inches of rain, with areas experiencing localized deluges of up to 6 inches.
Meteorologists advise caution for events such as Noah Kahan's concert, Sail Boston, and the World Cup match due to weather concerns.

title: Hampton Beach shooting suspect dead; two injured
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Two people were injured in a shooting at Hampton Beach, N.H., Sunday morning.
Suspect Tyshawn Cooper, 21, died from a self-inflicted gunshot during a police confrontation.
Police found the gunshot victims at 29 Ocean Blvd; the shooting remains under investigation.
